title: Design a globally distributed feature store that must serve fraud-scoring features with strong consistency across three regions (US, EU, APAC). Walk through your architecture.
prompt:
Design a globally distributed feature store that must serve fraud-scoring features with strong consistency across three regions (US, EU, APAC). Walk through your architecture.

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
For the specific features that need strong consistency — transaction velocity counters — I would not use a simple multi-master eventually-consistent store. I'd use a consensus-based replicated store with quorum writes across regions, keyed by customer ID, so writes only commit once a majority of regions acknowledge them, meaning wherever a read happens next, it sees the latest state. I'd use bounded-staleness read replicas for everything that doesn't need strict consistency, since strong consistency isn't free.

**Takeaway:** Fraud detection needs immediate consistency for velocity-type counters because a fraud ring can exploit a staleness window, spending in two regions within the replication lag before the system catches up — "just replicate everywhere eventually-consistent, we'll catch it in a batch job" misses the point of a real-time velocity check.
hint_notes: Only the specific strongly-consistent features (velocity counters) need quorum-write consensus replication; everything else can use bounded-staleness read replicas. A fraud ring can exploit an eventual-consistency staleness window across regions.
